title: 10 best herbal teas for itchy ears
intro_desc: Herbal teas for itchy ears are natural remedies made from a variety of herbs and plants that can help alleviate the discomfort of itchy ears. These teas typically contain ingredients like chamomile, calendula, and mullein, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help reduce itching and irritation in the ears. By drinking these herbal teas, individuals can experience relief from itchy ears and improve their overall quality of life. For example, a person who frequently experiences itchy ears due to allergies or ear infections can find relief by drinking herbal teas containing calendula and chamomile. These teas can help reduce inflammation and soothe the ears, providing much-needed relief and allowing individuals to go about their daily activities without discomfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with itchy ears because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can soothe irritation and reduce inflammation in the ear canal. The tea is also known to have antimicrobial and astringent properties, which can help to fight off any potential infections and dry out excess moisture in the ears, thereby alleviating itchiness. Additionally, yarrow tea may help to improve circulation and reduce inflammation in the Eustachian tubes, providing relief from ear discomfort and promoting overall ear health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory property, due to the presence of flavonoids, helps reduce swelling and inflammation in the ear canal, providing relief from itchiness.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial property of yarrow tea, owing to the presence of lactones and tannins, helps combat bacterial and fungal infections that can cause itchy ears.
Astringent: Yarrow tea's astringent property, caused by tannins, helps constrict and tighten ear tissues, reducing excess fluid and providing relief from itchiness.
Analgesic: The analgesic property of yarrow tea, attributed to the presence of volatile oils, helps alleviate pain and discomfort associated with itchy ears.
Antipruritic: Yarrow tea's antipruritic property, due to the presence of flavonoids, helps reduce itching and provides relief from irritation in the ear.
remedy_recipe: Heat 1 cup of water to boiling in a teapot or stovetop kettle.
Place 1 teaspoon of dried yarrow herb in an infuser or teapot strainer.
Pour the hot water over the yarrow herb and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the spent herb.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable temperature before using it as a warm compress on itchy ears.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Yarrow flowers are often used in teas for itchy ears due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe and alleviate discomfort.
Leaves: The leaves of the yarrow plant are rich in flavonoids and volatile oils, making them a popular choice for treating itchy ears by reducing inflammation and providing pain relief.
Seeds: Yarrow seeds contain essential oils and have been used in medicinal teas to help alleviate itchy ears by promoting circulation and reducing swelling.
Stems: The stems of the yarrow plant contain therapeutic compounds that can help treat itchy ears by reducing inflammation and providing relief from irritation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with itchy ears because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can soothe inflammation and reduce irritation in the ear canal. The tea is made from the flowers of the pot marigold plant, which contain flavonoids and carotenoids that have a calming effect on the skin. When used as a warm compress or applied topically as a drops, pot marigold tea can help alleviate itching and discomfort in the ears. Additionally, the tea may help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the ear, providing relief from itchiness and other symptoms. The natural properties of pot marigold tea make it a gentle and effective remedy for itchy ears.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea can reduce inflammation, which is important for itchy ears as inflammation can exacerbate ear discomfort and itchiness. The anti-inflammatory property comes from the presence of flavonoids and carotenoids.

Antimicrobial: Pot marigold tea has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent infections, which is crucial for itchy ears as infections can cause or worsen ear itchiness. The antimicrobial property is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and essential oils.

Analgesic: Pot marigold tea can alleviate pain, which is important for itchy ears as the itchiness can cause discomfort and pain. The analgesic property is due to the presence of flavonoids and triterpenoids.

Antihistamine: Pot marigold tea has antihistamine properties that can help reduce histamine production, which is important for itchy ears as histamine can cause itchiness. The antihistamine property is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and essential oils.

Emollient: Pot marigold tea can soothe and moisturize the ear canal, which is important for itchy ears as dryness can exacerbate ear itchiness. The emollient property is due to the presence of mucilage and saponins.
remedy_recipe: Infuse one teaspoon of dried pot marigold flowers in 250ml of boiling water for five minutes.
Steep the mixture for an additional ten minutes to extract maximum beneficial compounds.
Strain the liquid to remove flower particles.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Use a dropper to apply a few drops of the cooled tea into each ear, holding the affected ear upwards for a few seconds to allow the liquid to penetrate the ear canal.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are commonly used in teas for itchy ears due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant have a soothing effect, making them useful in teas to alleviate itchiness in ears.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds can be used in teas to help reduce ear inflammation and provide relief from itchiness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tea helps with itchy ears because it has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can alleviate irritation and discomfort in the ears. The tea's active compounds, such as chamazulene and bisabolol, have a calming effect on the body and can reduce inflammation in the ear canal. Additionally, chamomile has natural antiseptic and antimicrobial properties that can help to prevent infection and promote healing. By drinking chamomile tea or using it as a compress, the soothing and calming effects of the herb can help to alleviate itchiness and discomfort in the ears, providing relief and promoting overall ear health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tea helps reduce inflammation in the ear canal, which is important for itchy ears as inflammation can exacerbate itching and discomfort. This property is due to the presence of active constituents like chamazulene and bisabolol.

Antimicrobial: The tea has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent ear infections, which is crucial for itchy ears as infections can cause or worsen ear itching. The antimicrobial effects are attributed to the essential oils and flavonoids found in German chamomile.

Analgesic: German chamomile tea has pain-relieving properties, which is important for itchy ears as it can alleviate the discomfort associated with ear itching. The analgesic effects are mainly due to the presence of bisabolol and matricin.

Antihistamine: The tea has antihistamine properties that can help reduce histamine-induced inflammation and itching, making it helpful for itchy ears. The antihistamine effects are attributed to the flavonoids and essential oils present in German chamomile.

Anxiolytic: German chamomile tea has a calming effect, which is important for itchy ears as it can help reduce stress and anxiety related to ear discomfort. The anxiolytic effects are mainly due to the presence of apigenin and other flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Steep 2 teaspoons of dried German chamomile flowers in the boiled water for 10 minutes.
Strain the chamomile flowers from the water.
Allow the chamomile tea to cool down to a warm temperature.
Use a dropper to apply the warm chamomile tea to itchy ears for 5-10 minutes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of German chamomile are used to make medicinal teas for itchy ears due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: The leaves of German chamomile are used in teas to soothe itchy ears, as they contain compounds that have a calming effect on the skin.
Seeds: The seeds of German chamomile are used in teas to alleviate itchy ears, as they contain therapeutic oils that have a soothing effect on the ears.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower tea helps with itchy ears because it contains anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can alleviate ear discomfort. The tea's active compounds, such as echinacea and cichoric acid, have been shown to reduce inflammation and fight off ear infections, which can cause itchiness. Additionally, the tea's soothing properties can help to relieve irritation and provide a calming effect on the ears. Drinking the tea or using it as a compress can help to reduce itchiness and promote overall ear health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eastern purple coneflower tea helps reduce inflammation in the ear canal, alleviating itchiness, due to the presence of anti-inflammatory compounds like choline and flavonoids.

Antimicrobial: The tea inhibits the growth of bacteria and fungi in the ear, preventing infections that can cause itchy ears, thanks to the antimicrobial properties of compounds such as alkamides and eudesmanolides.

Analgesic: Eastern purple coneflower tea provides pain relief for itchy ears by numbing the area, which is particularly useful when itchiness is accompanied by discomfort or pain, due to the presence of analgesic constituents like sesquiterpene lactones.

Antioxidant: The tea's antioxidant properties help protect ear tissue from oxidative damage and support overall ear health, reducing the likelihood of itchy ears caused by inflammation or irritation, due to the presence of antioxidant compounds such as phenolic acids and flavonoids.

Immune-boosting: Eastern purple coneflower tea supports the immune system, enabling the body to fight off infections and reduce inflammation that can lead to itchy ears, thanks to the presence of immune-boosting constituents such as polysaccharides and alkamides.
remedy_recipe: Collect or purchase 2-3 dried Eastern purple coneflower (Echinacea purpurea) flower heads.
Chop the flower heads into small pieces, and place them in a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the chopped flower heads.
Allow the tea to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool down before using it as a warm compress for itchy ears.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in medicinal compounds, the flowers of the eastern purple coneflower are used to make teas that can help soothe itchy ears.
Leaves: High in antioxidants, the leaves of the eastern purple coneflower are used in teas to help alleviate irritation in the ears.
Seeds: The seeds of the eastern purple coneflower contain natural oils that can help reduce inflammation and itchiness in the ears when used in teas.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum tea helps with itchy ears because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off any potential infections in the ear canal. The tea is made from the leaves of the Tasmanian blue gum tree, which contain compounds that can soothe irritation and provide relief from itchiness. Additionally, the tea has a natural ability to remove earwax buildup, which can often cause discomfort and itching in the ears. Drinking the tea or using it as a ear drop can help alleviate the symptoms of itchy ears and promote overall ear health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Tasmanian blue gum tea helps reduce inflammation in the ear canal, providing relief from itchiness. This property is important because itchy ears are often a result of inflammation in the ear canal. The active constituents responsible for this property include terpinen-4-ol and α-pinene.

Antimicrobial: The tea has antimicrobial properties that help combat ear infections, which can cause itchiness. This property is important because ear infections can lead to itchiness and discomfort. The active constituents responsible for this property include terpinen-4-ol, 1,8-cineole, and α-pinene.

Analgesic: Tasmanian blue gum tea has pain-relieving properties that help alleviate ear pain associated with itchiness. This property is important because itchiness often comes with pain and discomfort. The active constituents responsible for this property include 1,8-cineole and α-pinene.

Antihistamine: The tea has natural antihistamine properties that help reduce histamine-induced itchiness in the ears. This property is important because histamine release can cause itchiness and inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property include globulol and viridiflorol.

Anti-pruritic: Tasmanian blue gum tea has anti-pruritic properties that help soothe and alleviate itchiness in the ears. This property is important because it directly addresses the main symptom of itchy ears. The active constituents responsible for this property include α-pinene and globulol.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried Tasmanian blue gum leaves.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Turn off the heat and allow the tea to cool for 30 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a glass, discarding the leaves.
Using a dropper, apply 2-3 drops of the cooled tea into each ear, holding the ear upright for 1-2 minutes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the Tasmanian blue gum plant are commonly used to make medicinal teas for itchy ears due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Flowers: The flowers of the Tasmanian blue gum plant are used in teas to soothe itchy ears, as they have natural antihistamine and anti-inflammatory effects.
Bark: The bark of the Tasmanian blue gum plant is often used in teas to treat itchy ears because of its astringent and antimicrobial properties.
Seeds: The seeds of the Tasmanian blue gum plant are included in teas for itchy ears because they contain essential oils with analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Alder buckthorn tea helps with itchy ears because it contains anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can soothe inflammation and fight off any potential infections causing the itchiness. The tea's herbal ingredients, such as alder bark and buckthorn berries, have been traditionally used to treat ear discomforts. The astringent properties found in the tea can help to dry out any excess fluid in the ears, reducing the chances of itchiness. Additionally, the tea's antioxidants can help to boost the immune system, promoting overall ear health and preventing future itchiness. Drinking alder buckthorn tea can provide a natural and effective way to alleviate itchy ears.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Alder buckthorn tea reduces inflammation in the ear canal, providing relief from itchiness caused by inflammation. This property is due to the presence of active constituents like flavonoids and tannins.
Antimicrobial: The tea inhibits the growth of microorganisms, helping to prevent ear infections that may cause itching. This is attributed to the presence of anthraquinones and phenolic acids.
Astringent: The astringent properties of alder buckthorn tea help to dry up excess earwax and fluid in the ear, reducing the likelihood of itchiness. This is due to the tannins present in the tea.
Analgesic: The tea has pain-relieving properties, helping to alleviate any discomfort or pain associated with itchy ears. This is attributed to the presence of anthraquinones.
Antihistamine: Alder buckthorn tea has natural antihistamine properties, helping to reduce histamine-induced itching in the ears. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 tablespoon of dried alder buckthorn bark and 1 quart of water.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried herbal flowers (such as chamomile or calendula) and let steep for an additional 5 minutes.
Strain the tea, then allow it to cool to a comfortable temperature.
Use a dropper to place a few drops of the tea in each ear, being careful to avoid touching the ear canal with the dropper.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, alder buckthorn flowers can help soothe itchy ears and reduce inflammation.
Leaves: Containing tannins and flavonoids, alder buckthorn leaves can provide astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, alleviating itchiness in the ears.
Bark: The bark of alder buckthorn has been used traditionally for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it suitable for treating itchy ears caused by infections.
Seeds: Alder buckthorn seeds are a great source of antioxidants and essential fatty acids, which can help reduce inflammation and relieve itchiness in the ears.
Buds: High in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, alder buckthorn buds can help soothe itchy ears and promote overall ear health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with itchy ears because it has a natural anti-inflammatory and soothing effect that can alleviate irritation in the ear canal. The active compounds in licorice, such as glycyrrhizin, work to reduce inflammation and swelling, which can help to relieve the discomfort associated with itchy ears. Additionally, licorice tea can help to soothe the mucous membranes in the ears, providing a further source of relief. The tea can be consumed orally, or the used tea bags can be applied topically to the ears to help reduce itching and irritation. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before using licorice tea for medicinal purposes, as long-term use or excessive consumption can lead to side effects.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea's anti-inflammatory property, due to its active constituent glycyrrhizin, helps reduce inflammation in the ear canal, alleviating itchiness.
Demulcent: The tea's demulcent properties, from its polysaccharides, form a soothing film over the inner ear to protect and relieve irritated ear canals.
Antimicrobial: Licorice tea's antimicrobial property, owing to its flavonoids and triterpenoids, helps combat potential microbial causes of ear itchiness.
Antihistamine: The antihistamine effect of licorice tea, attributed to its glycyrrhizin content, may help alleviate itchiness by inhibiting histamine release.
Analgesic: Licorice tea's analgesic property, due to its flavonoids, aids in reducing ear discomfort and itchiness.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of dried licorice root.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove licorice root pieces.
Allow the tea to cool down until it's warm, but not hot.
Use a dropper to apply the tea directly into each ear, holding it in place for 5 minutes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe itchy ears.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice plant have flavonoids that can help reduce inflammation and provide relief for itchy ears.
Seeds: Licorice seeds are used in traditional medicine to treat ear inflammation and provide relief from itchiness.
Flowers: The flowers of the licorice plant contain antioxidants that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate itchy ears.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's Wort tea helps with itchy ears because it contains anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can soothe inflammation and reduce irritation in the ear canal. The herb's active compounds, such as hypericin and hyperforin, have been shown to have a calming effect on the nerves, which can help alleviate the discomfort associated with itchy ears. Additionally, St. John's Wort tea has natural astringent properties that can help dry up excess fluid in the ears, reducing the likelihood of infection and providing relief from itchiness. Drinking St. John's Wort tea or using it as a compress can provide a natural and effective remedy for itchy ears.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's wort tea helps reduce inflammation in the ear canal, which can alleviate itching. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin and hyperforin.
Antimicrobial: The tea can help fight off any potential ear infections that may be causing itching. Hyperforin and flavonoids in St. John's wort contribute to its antimicrobial properties.
Analgesic: St. John's wort tea can help alleviate any pain or discomfort associated with itchy ears. Hypericin and hyperforin are believed to be responsible for the tea's analgesic effects.
Antihistamine: The tea may help reduce histamine release, which can help alleviate itching. Hypericin and hyperforin have been found to have antihistamine properties.

Anti-pruritic: St. John's wort tea can directly help reduce itching. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin and hyperforin, which have been found to have anti-pruritic effects.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 teaspoon of dried St. John's Wort flowers and leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Allow the herbs to steep for 10-15 minutes to extract the medicinal compounds.
Strain the tea to remove the plant material.
Warm the tea to a comfortable temperature.
Use a dropper to apply the warm tea to itchy ears, being careful to avoid getting it in the ear canal.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of St. John's wort are commonly used in making medicinal teas for itchy ears due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: The leaves of St. John's wort contain active compounds that can help alleviate itching and irritation in the ears when used in tea preparations.

Aerial parts: While not a specific part, it's worth noting that aerial parts of St. John's wort, such as stems and buds, are sometimes used together with flowers and leaves to make medicinal teas for itchy ears, providing a synergistic effect.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tea helps with itchy ears because it has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can alleviate irritation and discomfort in the ear canal. The tea's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have calming effects that can reduce itchiness and inflammation in the ear. Additionally, lavender tea has natural antiseptic and antimicrobial properties that can help prevent ear infections and promote ear health. Drinking lavender tea or using it as a compress can provide relief from itchy ears and improve overall ear health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English lavender tea's anti-inflammatory property, due to its richness in linalyl acetate and linalool, helps reduce inflammation in the ear canal and alleviate itching.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial property, attributed to the presence of eucalyptol and camphor, combats potential ear infections causing itchiness.
Analgesic: The analgesic effect of lavender tea, owing to its rosmarinic acid content, aids in pain relief from an itchy ear.
Antihistamine: The antihistamine property, a result of the high concentration of cineole, helps control histamine-induced itching in the ears.
Sedative: The sedative property of lavender tea, mainly due to its linalool and linalyl acetate content, promotes relaxation and reduces stress-related itching in the ears.
remedy_recipe: In a stovetop kettle, boil 2 cups of water for 5-7 minutes until it reaches a rolling boil.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried English lavender flowers to a tea infuser, then place it in a teapot.
Pour the boiling water over the lavender flowers in the teapot, then steep for 5-7 minutes.
After steeping, remove the tea infuser and discard the used lavender flowers.
Allow the lavender tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature before using it as a warm compress for itchy ears.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids, the flowers of English lavender have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them ideal for soothing itchy ears.
Leaves: Containing high levels of camphor and cineole, lavender leaves help alleviate ear discomfort and reduce inflammation.
Buds: Packed with essential oils, lavender buds provide quick relief from itchy ears due to their potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with itchy ears because it has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can soothe inflammation and irritation in the ear canal. The tea's active compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and citronellal, have a calming effect on the nervous system and can help reduce anxiety and tension, which may exacerbate ear discomfort. Additionally, lemon balm has natural antihistamine properties that can help alleviate allergic reactions that can cause itchy ears. Drinking lemon balm tea or using it as a compress can provide relief from itchy ears and promote overall ear health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lemon balm tea's anti-inflammatory property, due to the presence of rosmarinic acid, helps reduce inflammation in the ear canal, alleviating itchiness.
Antihistamine: Lemon balm contains antihistamine compounds that help reduce histamine-induced itching and inflammation in the ears.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of lemon balm, attributed to its essential oil content, may help prevent or treat ear infections causing itchiness.
Analgesic: Lemon balm's analgesic effects, caused by eugenol and other constituents, help alleviate ear pain associated with itchy ears.
Anxiolytic: Lemon balm's anxiolytic properties, due to rosmarinic acid and other compounds, may help reduce anxiety and stress related to ear itchiness, promoting overall relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Pick 2 tablespoons of fresh lemon balm leaves or use 1 tablespoon of dried leaves.
Rinse the lemon balm leaves under cold water to remove any dirt or impurities.
Place the leaves in a teapot and pour 1 cup of boiling water over them.
Let the tea steep for 5-10 minutes to allow the herbal properties to be released.
Strain the tea and discard the leaves before drinking.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm are commonly used in teas for itchy ears due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Flowers: The fragrant flowers of lemon balm contain compounds that help alleviate itching and reduce inflammation in the ears.
Stems: Although less potent than the leaves and flowers, the stems of lemon balm can still contribute to the tea's soothing effect on itchy ears.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat itchy ears with herbal teas is to steep dried herbs in hot water to release their active compounds. Chamomile, calendula, and lavender are all excellent choices due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. Once the tea has cooled, use a clean dropper to apply a few drops of the liquid into the affected ear, taking care to keep the liquid away from the ear canal. Allow the tea to sit in the ear for several minutes before draining. Repeat this process several times a day for best results.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to itchy ears that are treated with herbal teas include ear infections and inflammation. Herbal teas with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, such as chamomile and echinacea, can help alleviate these symptoms. Chamomile tea has soothing effects that can reduce inflammation and irritation in the ear canal, while echinacea tea can boost the immune system and fight off infections. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal teas as a treatment, especially if symptoms persist or worsen.
